commitments.text: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is continuously investing considerable efforts in medical services, tourism, education, renewable energy and energy efficiency technologies to enhance economic growth.
The Kingdom as a member of the Global Methane Pledge initiative will collaborate with other members to reduce global methane emissions by 30% by 2030 relative to 2020 levels.

measurability.reason: The commitment has a specific number, unit, target year, and baseline.

gap_findings.description: Saudi Arabia's NDC does not mention adaptation finance commitments.
gap_findings.ipcc_evidence: [WG2_Chapter17_DecisionMakingRisk_2022, p.48] Adaptation finance provided by international public mechanisms remains the core source of tracked flows in support of adaptation and resilience to developing countries.

gap_findings.description: Saudi Arabia's NDC mentions 'considerable efforts' in adaptation finance, but lacks quantification.
gap_findings.ndc_evidence: The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is continuously investing considerable efforts in medical services, tourism, education, renewable energy and energy efficiency technologies to enhance economic growth.
gap_findings.ipcc_evidence: [WG2_Chapter17_DecisionMakingRisk_2022, p.52] Adaptation finance provided by international public mechanisms remains the core source of tracked flows in support of adaptation and resilience to developing countries.

gap_findings.description: Saudi Arabia's NDC does not mention the need for adaptation finance, which is a key finding in the IPCC report.
gap_findings.ipcc_evidence: [WG2_Chapter17_DecisionMakingRisk_2022, p.48] Adaptation finance provided by international public mechanisms remains the core source of tracked flows in support of adaptation and resilience to developing countries.

details.summary: Saudi Arabia's NDC lacks commitment to adaptation finance, and its vague mention of 'considerable efforts' does not align with the IPCC's emphasis on the need for adaptation finance.

pass_c.country_summary: Saudi Arabia's NDC lacks commitment to adaptation finance, and its vague mention of 'considerable efforts' does not align with the IPCC's emphasis on the need for adaptation finance.
pass_c.policy_recommendation: Saudi Arabia should commit to increasing adaptation finance and provide a clear plan for achieving this goal, including quantifiable targets and timelines.
